case_name: Pomroy v. Hospital of the University of Pennsylvania

summary.brief: Pomroy v. Hospital of the University of Pennsylvania. Medical malpractice case involving oncology.

evidence_index.text: Mrs. Pomroy had a long history of gastrointestinal issues and multiple abdominal surgeries.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Fanelli recommended surgery. The trial testimony of George Pomroy, the decedent’s husband, in summarizing Dr. Fanelli’s advice, was: We went back and he told her it wasn’t cancerous and that it was rather large and he’s going to recommend us to a surgeon.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Mr. Pomroy testified that although Dr. Fanelli did not put a specific risk factor on the saline solution procedure, he definitely recommended against it.
evidence_index.speaker: George Pomroy

evidence_index.text: The uncontradicted testimony of Mr. Pomroy was that his wife feared having the polyp removed during a colonoscopy, even the saline colonoscopy method.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Following the surgery, Mrs. Pomroy suffered a series of complications that resulted in her unfortunate death.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: We conclude that the record is legally insufficient to support the jury’s verdict.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: As we conclude that the record cannot support the verdict on either liability or causation, Appellants’ claims on appeal merit relief.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: A JNOV should be entered only in a clear case.
evidence_index.speaker: Court reasoning

evidence_index.text: The standard of care, in my opinion, is that [the saline method endoscopically] should have been what he offered Ms. Pomroy.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Drew

initial_state.chief_complaint: Large, possibly cancerous polyp in colon
initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Mrs. Pomroy had a long history of gastrointestinal issues and multiple abdominal surgeries. She was informed by her gastroenterologist, Dr. Fanelli, about a large polyp in her colon, which was possibly cancerous.
initial_state.initial_working_problem: Large colon polyp with potential risk of cancer

requestables.name: Gastroenterology Consultation
requestables.description: Consultation with Dr. Fanelli regarding treatment options for the colon polyp

reveal.result_summary: Dr. Fanelli recommended surgery due to the size of the polyp and risk of perforation with colonoscopy.
reveal.clinical_significance: Surgical removal was advised over colonoscopic removal due to risk factors.
reveal.decision_impact: Led to referral to Dr. Rosato for surgical removal.

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents with a large colon polyp

timeline_phases.name: Decision Making
timeline_phases.description: Decision on treatment method for polyp removal

decision_points.clinical_context: Decision on method for polyp removal
decision_points.prompt: What method should be used to remove the large colon polyp?

options.description: Perform saline endoscopy to remove the polyp
options.clinical_reasoning: Saline endoscopy reduces risk of perforation but was not chosen due to patient's fear and polyp size.

options.description: Perform surgical removal of the polyp
options.clinical_reasoning: Surgical removal was chosen due to the size of the polyp and patient's preference.

actual_action_defendant.description: Surgical removal was performed by Dr. Rosato
actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Patient preferred surgery to avoid risk of perforation with colonoscopy.

expected_action_court.description: Saline endoscopy should have been considered more strongly
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert testimony suggested saline endoscopy as a safer alternative.

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: The expert testimony suggested that the saline method should have been pursued.
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Dr. Rosato should have insisted on the saline method or referred to another specialist.
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care as established by expert testimony.

patient_outcome.description: Mrs. Pomroy suffered complications post-surgery leading to her death.
patient_outcome.long_term_effects: Not applicable due to fatal outcome

key_findings: The record was legally insufficient to support the jury’s verdict.
No evidence of causation or breach of standard of care was established.

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Alleged failure to insist on saline endoscopy
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Not applicable as no breach was found

allegations.allegation: Failure to insist on saline endoscopy

court_findings.finding: No breach of duty or causation established
court_findings.legal_significance: Verdict reversed due to insufficient evidence
